message:
feat(runtime): add adaptive cancel-streak scheduling via EXP3/Hedge online policy

Three-lane scheduler gains a deterministic no-regret online policy for
adaptive cancel-streak limit selection. Uses EXP3-style multiplicative
weight updates across 5 arms [4, 8, 16, 32, 64] with epoch-based reward
signals derived from Lyapunov potential decrease, deadline pressure,
fairness (base/effective limit exceedances), and fallback cancel dispatch
rates. Normalized entropy of the arm distribution is tracked for
convergence diagnostics. Spectral health monitor integrated into the
scheduler for structural runtime awareness.

Worker panic path improved: cross-worker (foreign) wakers are now
properly cloned and woken after releasing the state lock instead of
being silently dropped with an error log, fixing potential task stalls
when a panicking task has waiters pinned to other workers.

IoDriver gains a public wake() method for cross-thread reactor nudging
after task injection. try_turn_with returns Option<usize> to
distinguish "another thread is polling" (None) from "polled, zero
events" (Some(0)), and removes the early-return on empty driver that
could miss events registered between the emptiness check and the poll.

Runtime config, builder, env vars, and TOML config all extended with
enable_adaptive_cancel_streak (default: true) and
adaptive_cancel_streak_epoch_steps (default: 128) knobs.

Region lifecycle tests in state.rs updated to check region removal
(is_none) rather than state comparison, matching the runtime's actual
behavior of removing closed regions from the arena.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
